primitive_inventory.exec_capture: NOT FOUND in hexa stage0 runtime (task premise unverified)
primitive_inventory.setenv: NOT FOUND in hexa stage0 runtime (env vars must be inlined as shell prefix in cmd string)

primitive_audit_method: grep across /Users/ghost/Dev/hexa-lang (stdlib, .hexa-cache compiled c, test/runner/builtins_test.hexa). Confirmed only exec / exec_with_status are exposed. Note: tool/phi_offset_fetch_cache.bash header explicitly states 'stage0 runtime blocks exec/exec_with_status' — runtime exposure may be gated per-tool by sandbox profile.

shell_features_used: while-IFS read loop with tab-delimited input
files.rationale: Drives long-running rclone uploads with streaming progress, single-instance pid-lock with EXIT trap, and pipeline status capture. Hexa exec() returns final stdout only — cannot stream rclone --stats output, cannot install signal traps, cannot probe PIDs with kill -0. Migration would degrade observability and lose the lock guard.

Pure-orchestration shape (parse args → fan-out per bucket → call rclone or aws CLI), but each branch needs reliable exit-code propagation through stderr-tee. Hexa exec_with_status would work for the per-bucket call, but env-passing for AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID / R2_ENDPOINT_URL would require inline 'KEY=val rclone …' shell prefixes — fragile vs current set -u + env inheritance. Defer pending a real setenv primitive.

Most logic is JSON emission + stat polling (hexa-eligible), but core work invokes lipo + cross-compile hexa build with stdin :quit REPL probe — needs PTY-style interactive stdin which exec(cmd) cannot supply. Atomic .tmp.$$ rename pattern is also bash-idiomatic.

shell_features_used: remote shell composition with cd && hexa parse && rm -f
files.rationale: Network-bound: scp + ssh-via-airgenome to remote host with timeouts. Each remote command composed as a multi-step ssh payload with cleanup. Hexa exec() lacks per-call timeout knob and would serialize-then-block on remote hang. Keep bash.

Header file itself documents WHY this is bash: 'active hexa stage0 runtime blocks exec/exec_with_status and exposes only a minimal string method surface, so the hexa side cannot run curl directly'. This bash IS the bridge for phi_offset_auto_detect.hexa. Migrating it back into hexa would defeat its purpose.

shell_features_used: child --selftest invocation with /tmp logfile + grep classification
heredoc emitting nested bash shim with escaped \$0 \$@
files.rationale: JSON emission and stat polling are hexa-eligible, but core value is generating an *executable bash shim* per package (heredoc with nested escapes). Hexa's string concatenation can produce the file content but writing executable + chmod +x + exec semantics is bash-native. Partial extraction yields ~30% LOC savings — not worth the surface change.

shell_features_used: child binary --selftest invocation with /tmp log capture
files.rationale: strip(1) is the load-bearing operation (cannot be replaced from hexa). Surrounding stat + json emit is hexa-eligible but the strip + post-strip selftest exec is bash-idiomatic. Keep bash.

Compression pipelines (zstd -19 -T0 streaming + shasum roundtrip verify), find -mtime, and stat-driven file iteration. Hexa exec() captures full stdout — cannot stream large file compression efficiently and cannot do reliable verify-then-delete with intermediate sha capture in one atomic flow.

shell_features_used: atomic .new file + mv -f swap with rollback log
files.rationale: Pure compression-pipeline tool with sha roundtrip gate. Critical-path shell pipeline (decompress | recompress) cannot be expressed as a single hexa exec() without losing pipefail semantics. Keep bash.

shell_features_used: ln -s symlink mgmt with readlink probe
files.rationale: Wraps launchctl(1) — macOS-specific service manager. Symlink semantics + launchctl load are bash-idiomatic. Per its header: 'NEVER run by the agent. The user must invoke it manually'. Keep bash.

Direct invocations of `sample` (macOS) and `perf record` (Linux) — multi-second sampling. Stdout parsing via multi-line awk programs that hexa cannot host. The synthetic --smoke branch alone could move to hexa, but splitting would fork the schema-emit code path.

shell_features_used: hf auth whoami output ANSI strip via sed
python3 heredoc for json verdict extract
files.rationale: H100 stop-gate critical path. Wraps runpodctl + hf CLI with 12-arg subcommands and ANSI stripping. set -euo pipefail behaviour is load-bearing. Per task: DO NOT trigger H100 launch — risk of regression here is unacceptable.

shell_features_used: JSONL progress ledger append + grep-based resume detection
files.rationale: Corpus shard pre-staging pipeline. Long-running tar+zstd, rclone uploads with resume from JSONL ledger. Same constraints as asset_archive_run.bash plus larger surface (#83 H100 dependency). Keep bash.

shell_features_used: rclone copy to R2 with size-resume
shasum -a 256 tokenizer integrity write-back to manifest
files.rationale: Largest tool — 438 LOC. Mirrors 4 base model weights HF→R2, computes tokenizer sha256, writes back to SSOT manifest. Per-file network retry + tokenizer integrity gate is bash-idiomatic. Critical-path for #83. Keep bash.

key_finding: Task premise referenced 'new exec_capture + setenv primitives' — repository search confirms hexa stage0 only exposes exec(cmd) and exec_with_status(cmd). No setenv primitive exists; no streaming/timeout knobs on exec; no signal traps. Under the conservative-when-in-doubt directive, every audited file uses at least one shell feature without a hexa equivalent (pipefail, traps, mapfile, find -mtime, here-strings, streaming subprocess output, kill -0 pid probes, scp/ssh w/ timeout, launchctl, awk multi-line distillation). Zero PURE_BRIDGE candidates → zero migrations performed. Re-audit when hexa exposes (1) exec_capture with separate stdout/stderr/rc, (2) setenv/getenv, (3) spawn with PID handle + signal API, (4) stream callbacks for long-running children.
recommendation: Track hexa runtime additions; revisit r2_lifecycle_apply.bash + universal_binary_build.bash + single_file_pkg.bash + bin_reduce.bash as first migration candidates once setenv + exec_capture land. h100_* and corpus_shard_recompress + log_rotation_zstd should remain bash indefinitely (pipeline-heavy).
